hard_fail_conditions: any external relativity/geometry law imported as a PREMISE (not merely named for comparison) anywhere in the active files
Gamma_R, the observer transforms, or any Lorentz-shaped result tagged [Th_coqc] instead of [Proposed internal bridge]
the SR/observer-cone sector (OA-06..OA-12) asserted as real physics, or asserted to be proven rather than conditional on the RD-neutrality gate
the O4 RD-neutrality gate (OA-06) treated as proven, derived, or ROOT_THEOREM rather than [Proposed internal gate]
graph front-speed v asserted equal to the physical speed of light c, or any coupling constant asserted derived from the root
any claim stronger than the declared tier FINITE_INTERNAL_CLOSURE (see CLAIM_BOUNDARY.json)
the operator-to-metric [Th_coqc] tag applied to any node other than OB-01's forward direction (spectrum-alone -> metric is explicitly forbidden)
Schwarzschild (OC-03) or Unruh (OC-04) presented as derived theorems rather than [DeclaredFormula] calculator identities
missing verifier (relativity_closure_v0_1.py and relativity_closure_v0_2.py), source_root snapshot, CLAIM_BOUNDARY.json, RULE_REGISTRY.json, ROOT_DAG_MASTER.md, CLOSURE_AUDIT.json, or run_tests.py
relativity_closure_v0_1.py modified from its shipped exact-rational form (e.g. floats introduced, checks removed, network calls added) -- it is the immutable v0.1 anchor
relativity_closure_v0_2.py introducing floats, removing checks, or adding network calls

OB-07 or OC-05 marked closed in v0.2 without the corresponding new finite internal witness (GC-01..GC-05 for OB-07/horizon; GD-01..GD-04 for the metric-source half) present in both relativity_closure_v0_2.py and RULE_REGISTRY.json
the Null-Transport Factorization Gate (GC-01) or the Geometry Stationarity Gate (GD-01..GD-04) tagged [Th_coqc] instead of [Proposed internal gate] / [Finite diagnostic]
N (the internal lapse), kappa_R, or S_Theta asserted as physical lapse / physical surface gravity / physical stress-energy respectively
unregistered active rule (a claim or equation appearing in code/docs with no corresponding rule_id in RULE_REGISTRY.json)
network or third-party runtime dependency introduced anywhere in the verification path
citing InfoLorentzInvariance / InfoQuantumRelativityUnification / InfoAnalysisLift / InfoQuantumGravityRootBridge as a root-native derivation (they import Minkowski/Schwarzschild as premises)
relabeling InfoTelegraphHorizonUnification's spine-crossover discriminant as node-8 lapse/redshift
note_on_tokens: These names may appear in prose ONLY as explicit comparisons/disclaimers (e.g. 'this is not the physical Lorentz transformation'). They fail the contract if activated as an imported PREMISE that any derivation or witness in relativity_closure_v0_1.py or RULE_REGISTRY.json depends on.
